## Implementation and Training Pipeline

### Experiment Specification Design

Each experiment is fully specified by a structured set of inputs covering the
dataset, feature subset, reward type, environment settings, and optimization
hyperparameters. No source-code changes are required to switch between
experimental variants. A pre-training validation stage checks path availability,
feature definitions, split feasibility, and column compatibility before any
computation begins, preventing wasted runs from invalid experimental
specifications. A condensed summary of the main specification is provided in
Appendix C.

### Feature Pipeline

Feature engineering is registry-driven: feature names resolve to computation
functions, allowing feature-set composition without editing training code. This
design is central to systematic feature subset comparisons. Normalization
parameters are estimated on the training split only for non-causal scalers. For
the main HFT experiment, features use causal running normalization: each split
is transformed chronologically, and the normalization state is reset at detected
session boundaries. This preserves causal integrity across both time and the
chronological split.

### Training Loop

The processed training split is used to construct the trading environment with
a continuous action space $a_t \in [-1, 1]$ representing target portfolio
exposure. Training follows the TD3 procedure described in Chapter 3, with
comparable implementations for DDPG and PPO for experimental comparison.
All three algorithms share a common training infrastructure: experience
collection, network updates, and evaluation. The primary differences are in the
update rules (deterministic policy gradient for DDPG/TD3, clipped surrogate for
PPO) and replay buffer usage (off-policy for DDPG/TD3, on-policy for PPO).

For TD3 and DDPG, experience is collected into a replay buffer, twin critics are
updated from sampled mini-batches, and the actor is updated every $d$ critic
steps via the deterministic policy gradient. Target networks track the main
networks through soft updates with coefficient $\tau$. Exploration noise is
applied during training and removed at evaluation time. For PPO, training follows
the on-policy paradigm: batches are collected using the current policy, multiple
epochs of updates are performed on each batch, and the clipped surrogate objective
constrains policy changes. The stochastic policy handles exploration through
entropy bonuses rather than explicit noise injection.

```{=latex}
\begin{algorithm}
\caption{TD3 Trading Experiment Workflow}
\begin{algorithmic}[1]
\Require Experiment specification (dataset, feature set, hyperparameters)
\Ensure Trained policy $\mu_\theta$, evaluation metrics

\State Load and validate experiment specification; check paths and split feasibility
\State Load L3 order book dataset; sort chronologically
\State Split data into train / validation / test subsets (chronological)
\State Initialise and validate feature pipeline on training split
\State Transform each split chronologically with causal session-aware normalization
\State Build training environment from processed training data
\State Initialise actor $\mu_\theta$, twin critics $Q_{\phi_1}$, $Q_{\phi_2}$,
       target networks $\mu_{\theta'}$, $Q_{\phi_1'}$, $Q_{\phi_2'}$,
       replay buffer $\mathcal{D}$

\For{each step $t = 1, \ldots, \texttt{max\_steps}$}
    \State $a_t \leftarrow \mu_\theta(s_t) + \varepsilon$, \quad $\varepsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\, \sigma_{\text{explore}})$
    \State Execute $a_t$; observe $r_t$, $s_{t+1}$; store $(s_t, a_t, r_t, s_{t+1})$ in $\mathcal{D}$
    \State Sample mini-batch $\mathcal{B} \sim \mathcal{D}$
    \State Compute smoothed target action: $\tilde{a}' \leftarrow \mu_{\theta'}(s') + \text{clip}\!\left(\mathcal{N}(0,\sigma_{\text{noise}}),\, \pm c\right)$
    \State Compute Bellman target: $y \leftarrow r + \gamma \cdot \min_{i=1,2} Q_{\phi_i'}(s',\, \tilde{a}')$
    \State Update both critics by minimising $\mathcal{L}_{\text{Smooth-L1}}\!\left(y - Q_{\phi_i}(s, a)\right)$
    \If{$t \bmod \texttt{policy\_delay} = 0$}
        \State Update actor: $\theta \leftarrow \theta + \alpha \nabla_\theta Q_{\phi_1}(s,\, \mu_\theta(s))$
        \State Soft-update targets: $\phi_i' \leftarrow \tau\phi_i + (1-\tau)\phi_i'$; \; $\theta' \leftarrow \tau\theta + (1-\tau)\theta'$
    \EndIf
    \State Checkpoint and log metrics at configured intervals
\EndFor

\State Evaluate deterministic policy $\mu_\theta$ on test split
\State Compute financial performance metrics from realised return series
\end{algorithmic}
\end{algorithm}
```

```{=html}
<pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border:1px solid #ddd; padding:1em; font-size:0.88em; line-height:1.5;">
<strong>Algorithm 1</strong> TD3 Trading Experiment Workflow
<em>Require:</em> Experiment specification (dataset, feature set, hyperparameters)
<em>Ensure:</em>  Trained policy μ<sub>θ</sub>, evaluation metrics

 1: Load and validate experiment specification; check paths and split feasibility
 2: Load L3 order book dataset; sort chronologically
 3: Split data into train / validation / test subsets (chronological)
 4: Initialise and validate feature pipeline on training split
 5: Transform each split chronologically with causal session-aware normalization
 6: Build training environment from processed training data
 7: Initialise actor μ<sub>θ</sub>, twin critics Q<sub>φ1</sub>, Q<sub>φ2</sub>,
    target networks μ<sub>θ'</sub>, Q<sub>φ1'</sub>, Q<sub>φ2'</sub>, replay buffer 𝒟
 8: <strong>for</strong> each step t = 1, …, max_steps <strong>do</strong>
 9:   a<sub>t</sub> ← μ<sub>θ</sub>(s<sub>t</sub>) + ε,  ε ~ 𝒩(0, σ<sub>explore</sub>)
10:   Execute a<sub>t</sub>; observe r<sub>t</sub>, s<sub>t+1</sub>; store (s<sub>t</sub>, a<sub>t</sub>, r<sub>t</sub>, s<sub>t+1</sub>) in 𝒟
11:   Sample mini-batch ℬ ~ 𝒟
12:   Compute smoothed target action: ã' ← μ<sub>θ'</sub>(s') + clip(𝒩(0, σ<sub>noise</sub>), ±c)
13:   Compute Bellman target: y ← r + γ · min<sub>i=1,2</sub> Q<sub>φi'</sub>(s', ã')
14:   Update both critics by minimising ℒ<sub>Smooth-L1</sub>(y − Q<sub>φi</sub>(s, a))
15:   <strong>if</strong> t mod policy_delay = 0 <strong>then</strong>
16:     Update actor: θ ← θ + α∇<sub>θ</sub>Q<sub>φ1</sub>(s, μ<sub>θ</sub>(s))
17:     Soft-update targets: φi' ← τφi + (1−τ)φi';  θ' ← τθ + (1−τ)θ'
18:   <strong>end if</strong>
19:   Checkpoint and log metrics at configured intervals
20: <strong>end for</strong>
21: Evaluate deterministic policy μ<sub>θ</sub> on test split
22: Compute financial performance metrics from realised return series
</pre>
```

```{=latex}
\begin{algorithm}
\caption{DDPG Trading Experiment Workflow}
\begin{algorithmic}[1]
\Require Experiment specification (dataset, feature set, hyperparameters)
\Ensure Trained policy $\mu_\theta$, evaluation metrics

\State Load and validate experiment specification; check paths and split feasibility
\State Load L3 order book dataset; sort chronologically
\State Split data into train / validation / test subsets (chronological)
\State Initialise and validate feature pipeline on training split
\State Transform each split chronologically with causal session-aware normalization
\State Build training environment from processed training data
\State Initialise actor $\mu_\theta$, critic $Q_\phi$,
       target networks $\mu_{\theta'}$, $Q_{\phi'}$,
       replay buffer $\mathcal{D}$

\For{each step $t = 1, \ldots, \texttt{max\_steps}$}
    \State $a_t \leftarrow \mu_\theta(s_t) + \varepsilon$, \quad $\varepsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\, \sigma_{\text{explore}})$
    \State Execute $a_t$; observe $r_t$, $s_{t+1}$; store $(s_t, a_t, r_t, s_{t+1})$ in $\mathcal{D}$
    \State Sample mini-batch $\mathcal{B} \sim \mathcal{D}$
    \State Compute Bellman target: $y \leftarrow r + \gamma \cdot Q_{\phi'}(s',\, \mu_{\theta'}(s'))$
    \State Update critic by minimising $\mathcal{L}_{\text{Smooth-L1}}\!\left(y - Q_\phi(s, a)\right)$
    \State Update actor: $\theta \leftarrow \theta + \alpha_\theta \nabla_\theta Q_\phi(s,\, \mu_\theta(s))$
    \State Soft-update targets: $\phi' \leftarrow \tau\phi + (1-\tau)\phi'$; \; $\theta' \leftarrow \tau\theta + (1-\tau)\theta'$
    \State Checkpoint and log metrics at configured intervals
\EndFor

\State Evaluate deterministic policy $\mu_\theta$ on test split
\State Compute financial performance metrics from realised return series
\end{algorithmic}
\end{algorithm}
```

```{=html}
<pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border:1px solid #ddd; padding:1em; font-size:0.88em; line-height:1.5;">
<strong>Algorithm 2</strong> DDPG Trading Experiment Workflow
<em>Require:</em> Experiment specification (dataset, feature set, hyperparameters)
<em>Ensure:</em>  Trained policy μ<sub>θ</sub>, evaluation metrics

 1: Load and validate experiment specification; check paths and split feasibility
 2: Load L3 order book dataset; sort chronologically
 3: Split data into train / validation / test subsets (chronological)
 4: Initialise and validate feature pipeline on training split
 5: Transform each split chronologically with causal session-aware normalization
 6: Build training environment from processed training data
 7: Initialise actor μ<sub>θ</sub>, critic Q<sub>φ</sub>,
    target networks μ<sub>θ'</sub>, Q<sub>φ'</sub>, replay buffer 𝒟
 8: <strong>for</strong> each step t = 1, …, max_steps <strong>do</strong>
 9:   a<sub>t</sub> ← μ<sub>θ</sub>(s<sub>t</sub>) + ε,  ε ~ 𝒩(0, σ<sub>explore</sub>)
10:   Execute a<sub>t</sub>; observe r<sub>t</sub>, s<sub>t+1</sub>; store (s<sub>t</sub>, a<sub>t</sub>, r<sub>t</sub>, s<sub>t+1</sub>) in 𝒟
11:   Sample mini-batch ℬ ~ 𝒟
12:   Compute Bellman target: y ← r + γ · Q<sub>φ'</sub>(s', μ<sub>θ'</sub>(s'))
13:   Update critic by minimising ℒ<sub>Smooth-L1</sub>(y − Q<sub>φ</sub>(s, a))
14:   Update actor: θ ← θ + α<sub>θ</sub>∇<sub>θ</sub>Q<sub>φ</sub>(s, μ<sub>θ</sub>(s))
15:   Soft-update targets: φ' ← τφ + (1−τ)φ';  θ' ← τθ + (1−τ)θ'
16:   Checkpoint and log metrics at configured intervals
17: <strong>end for</strong>
18: Evaluate deterministic policy μ<sub>θ</sub> on test split
19: Compute financial performance metrics from realised return series
</pre>
```

```{=latex}
\begin{algorithm}
\caption{PPO Trading Experiment Workflow}
\begin{algorithmic}[1]
\Require Experiment specification (dataset, feature set, hyperparameters)
\Ensure Trained policy $\pi_\theta$, evaluation metrics

\State Load and validate experiment specification; check paths and split feasibility
\State Load L3 order book dataset; sort chronologically
\State Split data into train / validation / test subsets (chronological)
\State Initialise and validate feature pipeline on training split
\State Transform each split chronologically with causal session-aware normalization
\State Build training environment from processed training data
\State Initialise stochastic actor $\pi_\theta$ (TanhNormal), value network $V_\phi$

\For{each batch iteration $k = 1, \ldots, \texttt{max\_steps} / \texttt{frames\_per\_batch}$}
    \State Collect trajectory $\mathcal{T}_k$ of \texttt{frames\_per\_batch} steps using $\pi_\theta$
    \State Compute advantage estimates $\hat{A}_t$ via GAE using $V_\phi$ on $\mathcal{T}_k$
    \For{each PPO epoch $j = 1, \ldots, K$}
        \State Sample mini-batch $\mathcal{B} \sim \mathcal{T}_k$
        \State Compute probability ratio: $r_t(\theta) \leftarrow \pi_\theta(a_t \mid s_t) / \pi_{\theta_{\text{old}}}(a_t \mid s_t)$
        \State Compute clipped objective: $\mathcal{L}^{\text{CLIP}} \leftarrow \mathbb{E}\!\left[\min\!\left(r_t \hat{A}_t,\; \text{clip}(r_t, 1\pm\epsilon)\hat{A}_t\right)\right]$
        \State Compute value loss: $\mathcal{L}^{V} \leftarrow \mathcal{L}_{\text{L2}}\!\left(V_\phi(s) - V^{\text{target}}\right)$
        \State Compute entropy bonus: $\mathcal{L}^{H} \leftarrow c_1 \mathcal{H}(\pi_\theta(\cdot \mid s))$
        \State Update $\theta$, $\phi$ jointly: minimise $-\mathcal{L}^{\text{CLIP}} + c_2 \mathcal{L}^{V} - \mathcal{L}^{H}$
    \EndFor
    \State Checkpoint and log metrics at configured intervals
\EndFor

\State Evaluate policy mode $\tanh(\mu_\theta(s))$ on test split
\State Compute financial performance metrics from realised return series
\end{algorithmic}
\end{algorithm}
```

```{=html}
<pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border:1px solid #ddd; padding:1em; font-size:0.88em; line-height:1.5;">
<strong>Algorithm 3</strong> PPO Trading Experiment Workflow
<em>Require:</em> Experiment specification (dataset, feature set, hyperparameters)
<em>Ensure:</em>  Trained policy π<sub>θ</sub>, evaluation metrics

 1: Load and validate experiment specification; check paths and split feasibility
 2: Load L3 order book dataset; sort chronologically
 3: Split data into train / validation / test subsets (chronological)
 4: Initialise and validate feature pipeline on training split
 5: Transform each split chronologically with causal session-aware normalization
 6: Build training environment from processed training data
 7: Initialise stochastic actor π<sub>θ</sub> (TanhNormal), value network V<sub>φ</sub>
 8: <strong>for</strong> each batch iteration k = 1, …, max_steps / frames_per_batch <strong>do</strong>
 9:   Collect trajectory 𝒯<sub>k</sub> of frames_per_batch steps using π<sub>θ</sub>
10:   Compute advantage estimates Â<sub>t</sub> via GAE using V<sub>φ</sub> on 𝒯<sub>k</sub>
11:   <strong>for</strong> each PPO epoch j = 1, …, K <strong>do</strong>
12:     Sample mini-batch ℬ ~ 𝒯<sub>k</sub>
13:     Compute probability ratio: r<sub>t</sub>(θ) ← π<sub>θ</sub>(a<sub>t</sub>|s<sub>t</sub>) / π<sub>θ_old</sub>(a<sub>t</sub>|s<sub>t</sub>)
14:     Compute clipped objective: ℒ<sup>CLIP</sup> ← 𝔼[min(r<sub>t</sub>Â<sub>t</sub>, clip(r<sub>t</sub>, 1±ε)Â<sub>t</sub>)]
15:     Compute value loss: ℒ<sup>V</sup> ← ℒ<sub>L2</sub>(V<sub>φ</sub>(s) − V<sup>target</sup>)
16:     Compute entropy bonus: ℒ<sup>H</sup> ← c<sub>1</sub> ℋ(π<sub>θ</sub>(·|s))
17:     Update θ, φ jointly: minimise −ℒ<sup>CLIP</sup> + c<sub>2</sub>ℒ<sup>V</sup> − ℒ<sup>H</sup>
18:   <strong>end for</strong>
19:   Checkpoint and log metrics at configured intervals
20: <strong>end for</strong>
21: Evaluate policy mode tanh(μ<sub>θ</sub>(s)) on test split
22: Compute financial performance metrics from realised return series
</pre>
```

The notation follows the conventions established in Chapter 3.
For TD3 and DDPG: $\mu_\theta$ is the deterministic actor; $Q_\phi$ (DDPG) and $Q_{\phi_1}$, $Q_{\phi_2}$ (TD3) are the critic networks; primed symbols denote slowly updated target networks; $\mathcal{D}$ is the replay buffer; $\varepsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma_{\text{explore}})$ is training-only exploration noise; $\tau$ is the soft-update coefficient.
For PPO: $\pi_\theta$ is the stochastic actor (TanhNormal distribution); $V_\phi$ is the state value network; $\hat{A}_t$ are GAE advantage estimates; $r_t(\theta)$ is the probability ratio between new and old policies; $\epsilon$ is the clipping threshold; $c_1$, $c_2$ are the entropy and value loss coefficients; $K$ is the number of PPO epochs per batch.
$\gamma$ is the discount factor and $\alpha$ the learning rate across all three algorithms. Concrete values are listed in Appendix B.

### Evaluation

Final performance is reported on the held-out test split using a dedicated
evaluation environment. Two output streams are kept strictly separate. First is the
training reward, which serves as an optimization diagnostic. Second is the realized portfolio return
series from which financial metrics are computed.

Conflating these would invalidate
the empirical comparison. For example, treating cumulative DSR as a Sharpe ratio
estimate would be incorrect. Metrics derived from the test rollout are described
in Chapter 6.
